title: chrysothamnus viscidiflorus subsp. lanceolatus
intro: Chrysothamnus viscidiflorus subsp. lanceolatus, also known as lanceleaf greasewood, is a shrubby plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small, white flowers and sticky foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of lanceleaf greasewood include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and even exhibit antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of lanceleaf greasewood can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
